title: rubus serpens subsp. brevistamineus
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. brevistamineus, also known as the creeping raspberry or small-flowered dewberry, is a sub-species of the Rubus serpens plant, a type of wild raspberry native to North America. The leaves and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus serpens subsp. brevistamineus include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams.
